expected_effect: Keep the near-breakout raw edge of the apr18 anchors while spending opportunity budget only in windows that look dominated by uninformed flow rather than by fast price-jump risk.
expected_failure_signature: If the admission test is too strict, low-retail and low-volatility slices stay flat while anti-arb metrics improve only trivially and the branch simply replays overpriced calm flow.
expected_win_condition: Lift the weak calm slices of screen_0006 without reopening cheap-mode leakage, so the official screen incumbent clears both the promotion margin and the 480 breakout gate.
hidden_coupling_removed: Passive refill should not be funded by the same calm-memory signal that recenters fair mid; release budget only when the expected value of passive fills remains positive after accounting for jump hazard and stale quote risk.
rationale: Use the screen_0006 passive-refill anchor and the screen_0008 anti-arb anchor as context, but let safe-side refill spend only when expected passive fill value stays positive after a jump-risk and latency screen, instead of opening from calm confirmations alone.
title: Fill-value admission budget
why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes the admission rule for opportunity spend from calm-state accumulation to positive expected fill value under latency risk, rather than narrowing the same refill window.
